description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe featuring a broad, symmetrical blade on each side, both made of a metallic material with a slightly weathered, matte finish. The blades are semicircular, forming a crescent shape with sharp outer edges and a central notch at the top, giving the axe a formidable appearance. The handle is straight, cylindrical, and appears to be crafted from wood, with a smooth, natural finish and no visible grip enhancements or wrappings. The transition between the handle and the blade is seamless, with the handle passing through the central eye of the blade, suggesting a traditional mounting style. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, engravings, or branding. This axe would be suitable for display, theatrical use, or as a prop in urban or historical-themed environments.
description: A double-headed battle axe with broad, crescent-shaped metal blades and a straight wooden handle, featuring a simple, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, symmetrical metal blades and the full width of the axe head.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical wooden handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, identical to the front, displaying both blades and the handle's back.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and straight handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical wooden handle with a smooth finish and round cross-section; no visible grip features or curvature.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, crescent-shaped steel blades with sharp outer edges and a central notch at the top; symmetrical and functional with no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional, with the handle passing through the central eye of the blade.
